April 20, 2026

HYPE Year 1: the coming RWA and stablecoin wave on Hyperliquid

HYPE found its use case. Stablecoins haven't — yet.

How Hyperliquid got built — perps, then EVM, then RWAs

Over 2.5 years of running the orderbook produced $100B+ in monthly perp volume, the deepest on-chain book in crypto, and a live protocol generating real fee revenue before the EVM was built. Hyperliquid inverted the Ethereum / Solana order of operations:

  • Phase 1 — Perps (2023). Two and a half years of running the orderbook produced the deepest on-chain perp venue in crypto.
  • Phase 2 — HyperEVM (Feb 18, 2025). Protocols arrived next to the orderbook, and a portion of HYPE was allocated to build the EVM infrastructure — LSTs, lending, CDPs, DEXs — all built against a live book instead of an empty chain.
  • Phase 3 — HIP-3 builder perps (Oct 2025). Independent deployers started listing their own perp markets — equities, FX, commodities, indices — all settling into the same validator set. Six months in, HIP-3 is pushing ~$1.90B in aggregate open interest and ~37% of Hyperliquid's monthly perp volume. Stablecoins on HyperEVM rode the same wave: $60M at HIP-3 launch → $1.59B today, 25× in six months — and most of it is still looking for a yield.

For scale, chain-only revenue trailing 12 months: Hyperliquid $980M on $6.69B TVL; Ethereum ~$140M on ~$45B; Base ~$65M on ~$3–5B; Solana ~$38M on ~$10B. Hyperliquid is ~7× Ethereum and ~25× Solana on ~15% of Ethereum's TVL, because the fees are earned at the layer that has an orderbook — and that layer is the chain itself. With HIP-3, Hyperliquid turned that orderbook into a product surface. Protocols can now launch their own perps, spot markets, and (coming soon) prediction markets directly on Hyperliquid's matching engine.

The chain scaled $1.47B → $6.69B

Chain-level TVL ran $1.47B → $9.46B peak → $6.69B today, and the composition shifted with each phase. HyperEVM didn't ramp — it erupted: $0 → $677M on launch day → $2.79B peak in September 2025 → $1.44B today (+113% launch-to-now, through a full cycle). The orderbook side compounded in parallel: $2.38B → $5.25B (+120%) over roughly the same window, because HyperEVM's seed protocols (Felix, HyperLend, KittenSwap, Kinetiq) deployed into a chain that already had $5B of USDC on the book and a tradeable HYPE spot market — collateral and demand existed from day one.

HYPE priced like cash flow — ~10× PE, 3.4 bps take rate

HIP-3 looked like it would crush the blended take rate. Deployers run growth-mode fees — below native crypto-perp tiers to pull volume into equities, FX, commodities, and indices, and that extra volume drops straight into the denominator. Bps rose anyway: 2025 pre-HIP-3 corrected take rate ran ~3.21 bps; post-HIP-3 it sits at ~3.92 bps median, 3.41 bps today. HIP-3 isn't a discount on the first fee layer — it's a second fee layer on the same book, so every new listing adds to the numerator faster than it adds to the denominator. The Assistance Fund buys back HYPE with those fees and the market prices the token on that cash flow — which is why the price panel tracks daily fees step-for-step. ~$1.18B trailing gross fees / $980M net revenue puts Hyperliquid top-five across all on-chain protocols, and a ~10× PE against that line is what a monetizing protocol looks like when cash flow actually accrues to the token. Rearview: HYPE +221% from Dec 5, 2024; BTC −19%, ETH −37%, SOL −63%. Forward: 10× on a fee line widening its base — on a per-trade rate HIP-3 was supposed to compress but didn't — is the cleanest cash-flow setup in crypto.

Who actually earned the $1.18B? HyperCore Perps took ~91%. That line is the native orderbook — BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE and every other listed perp — and it prints in the $60–130M/month range independently of what's happening on HyperEVM. HIP-3 deployers are the second surface. Six months in, Tradexyz plus its smaller peers are already running an estimated ~$29M T13 — and that number is compounding fast enough to pass HyperCore Spot ($40M T13) within a quarter. HyperEVM is the third surface at ~$22M, and inside it the composition is already sharpening: DEXs hold 8% of HyperEVM TVL and out-earned lending + LSTs + yield aggregators combined in the last 30 days — $437k on $244M of TVL (~2.2% annualized take), versus lending at 0.16% and LSTs at 0.15%. Active trading monetizes; parked collateral doesn't. The cohort that is getting paid on the chain — Tradexyz (HIP-3), Felix (CDPs + tokenized equities), Project X, Unit (native-BTC, native-ETH wraps), Ondo (tokenized Treasuries and equities) — is exactly the cohort providing the spot liquidity that the next asset class will need. HYPE paid for the plumbing — the EVM allocation built out LSTs, lending, CDPs, and DEXs — and that infrastructure is already live for the dollars that are about to use it at scale.

Where HYPE lives — the 642M that's actually deployed

Staking is the gravity well. 63% of active HYPE is staked — earning ~2–2.5% APR paid in HYPE from the unemitted reserve (the 38.89% future-emissions bucket), not from orderbook fees. Orderbook fees fund the Assistance Fund instead, which has bought back 43M HYPE (~$1.2B at ~$28 avg). On April 9, 2026, daily buybacks overtook daily emissions — net supply now shrinks every day.

HYPE on HyperEVM paid for the plumbing. The EVM allocation funded the first wave — LSTs, lending, CDPs, DEXs — because HYPE demand justified it. But the same staking gravity that gave the chain its cash-flow engine capped what HyperEVM could pay on stablecoins: with 63% of HYPE preferring native staking, the borrow side that was supposed to feed dollar yield never filled.

HyperEVM HYPE round-tripped. Zero at launch → 66M peak in Oct 2025 → 30M (~5% of active) today. Tokens walked back to native staking the week HIP-3 launched, chasing HYPE-paid rewards instead of dollar-paid yield HyperEVM never built. But the protocols that built the first wave aren't sitting still: Felix ran CDPs against HYPE and has since partnered with Ondo to list tokenized equities; other CDP and DEX teams are queuing up their own HIP-3 perp markets. And corewriter now lets those HyperEVM protocols tap the orderbook directly — routing stablecoin vaults, LPs, and basis strategies straight into Hyperliquid's book to generate yield on-chain, without needing a HYPE-collateralized borrow to do it.

Part 2

The next wave — stablecoins, RWAs, haUSDC

HyperEVM — HYPE round-tripped, $1.59B of dollars showed up idle

The chart is the phased build in one frame. Phase 1 (orderbook) compounded HYPE cash flow; Phase 2 (HyperEVM) ran a full HYPE round-trip on its own supply — 0 → 99M peak Sep 2025 → 78M today — as tokens walked back to native staking for emissions-paid rewards; and at Phase 3 (HIP-3) launch, dollars showed up: $1.59B of ERC-20 stables, 25× in six months, while HYPE-denominated TVL drew down.

Split by venue, the dollars favor the book. $5.10B stablecoin on Hyperliquid, ~93% USDC: $3.51B as perp margin on the orderbook (69%), $1.59B as ERC-20 on HyperEVM (31%). The EVM side sits because borrow demand is parked in staking — HyperLend stablecoin supply is ~$24M combined (USDC 3.74% / $21.2M, USDT0 5.76% / $2.5M); Felix feUSDC runs 7–9% on $38M but feUSD is a CDP minted against HYPE. Every HYPE-collateral product shipped in year one hits the same ceiling.

And mainnets already pay. sUSDe 3.67% on $2.41B, Maple USDC 4.82%, Aave v3 USDC 3–5%. USDC on Ethereum has no reason to bridge for what it earns at home — so the pull has to come from what Hyperliquid has and mainnets don't: a native orderbook on the same chain, reachable from HyperEVM through corewriter without a CEX leg, with HIP-3 stacked on top.

HIP-3 and RWAs — the capacity the stables have been waiting for

HIP-3 is a second product line on the same rails. Zero at launch in October 2025 → ~$47B April MTD (pacing March's $68B) → ~37% of Hyperliquid's monthly perp volume with ~$1.90B aggregate OI. XYZ runs ~93% on 65 tickers across equities, commodities, FX and indices; dreamcash, Ventuals (Anthropic, OpenAI, SpaceX), Markets by Kinetiq, Felix Exchange and HyENA fill the rest.

The spot leg is landing in parallel on HyperEVM. Felix and Ondo shipped tokenized equities last quarter; THBILL (Treasuries) and XAUT0 (tokenized gold) are on the book; UBTC (Unit's native-BTC wrap) is ~$249M across 9,281 holders. Each ticker becomes a basis trade the moment both legs exist on the same chain.

The funding dispersion isn't theoretical. WTI crude and Brent printed −85% and −55% annualized over the last 30 days — shorts paying longs on real-world hedger flow. Ventuals' private-co perps run +99% ANTHROPIC, +32% SPACEX, +10% OPENAI — sustained long-pays-short with no public equity to short against. Commodity-exposed equities (HIMS +49%, SKHX +41%, MU +13%) print what crypto majors never do. Crypto funding is one-sided and compressed to single digits by mature arb; HIP-3 is two-sided and wide, and every new market listed expands the surface.

Bottom line — haUSDC is the uncapped wrapper

haUSDC is the wrapper that doesn't need HYPE. A USDC vault on HyperEVM, connected via corewriter straight to Hyperliquid's orderbook, earning from three sources — none of which cap on HYPE-collateral supply:

  • Perp-funding basis on orderbook majors — $5.67B MAIN + $1.90B HIP-3 = $7.58B of addressable OI, growing with every new listing.
  • Cash-and-carry on HIP-3 RWAs — long Felix / Ondo tokenized equity spot, short the matching HIP-3 perp. Funding on HIP-3 runs ~12× the magnitude of crypto majors, so each new ticker adds more capturable yield than another BTC/ETH pair would.
  • Treasury floor + synthetic-dollar basis — THBILL as a 4–5% base, sUSDe-style capture on top, except the perp leg clears in the same block as the spot leg instead of bridging to a CEX.
Trailing yield and scale for the reference delta-neutral dollar wrapper versus the HyperEVM surface it's about to compete on. Sources: DefiLlama yields, Hyperliquid funding history.

sUSDe (Ethena)Ethereum3.69%4.26%5.12%$2.47BCEX perp OI

Spark Savings USDCEthereum3.65%~4%~4.5%$0.49BSky DSR
Compound v3 USDCEthereum2.60%~3%~4%$0.12BETH/BTC borrow demand

haUSDC (via corewriter → orderbook)Hyperliquid7–10%* target (pre-launch)addressable: $5.67B MAIN + $1.90B HIP-3 OIHL OI + HIP-3 listings

*haUSDC is pre-launch — no live yield data. The 7–10% target range is based on blended long-spot / short-perp funding capture on BTC+ETH Hyperliquid perps (trailing 365d ≈ 8.3%), plus Treasury floor and HIP-3 equity basis as additional sources.

The table splits cleanly: every HYPE-tied row sits at tens of millions; every row that scales with the book sits at billions. sUSDe runs 3.69% on $2.47B off CEX funding — below the 7–10% band haUSDC targets via HL basis + Treasury + HIP-3, on an orderbook native to the chain. And the mechanic is reflexive: haUSDC flows become resting liquidity on the book, tighter books pull bigger traders, bigger traders widen funding dispersion, which lifts the yield that brought the dollars in the first place. Because the haUSDC share token is an ERC-20 on HyperEVM, the same dollar keeps working elsewhere — collateral on HyperLend or Felix, PT/YT on Pendle, margin against a HIP-3 perp.

Harmonix already shipped the HYPE-denominated version: haHYPE and hakHYPE are live. haUSDC is the stablecoin sibling — pointed at the gap the next wave creates.

The chain already built the engine. What's missing is the wrapper that lets a dollar hold the receipt. Twelve months from now, the question isn't whether USDC on Hyperliquid finds a yield — it's whether it does so through haUSDC before anything else.
